geopolitics_summary: Iran war disrupting Hormuz; EU weakening methane rules under US pressure; Libya gas pivotal for Italy supply.

themes.headline: Italy seeks Libyan gas to offset Iran war supply shortages
themes.why_it_matters: Direct Italy-Libya pipeline offers alternative to disrupted ME/Iran flows; production ramp-up difficult, limiting TTF support. Key for southern EU supply curve.

themes.headline: EU Commission weakens methane enforcement rules under US pressure
themes.why_it_matters: Relaxed methane rules reduce compliance cost for gas producers; lower incentive to invest in efficiency/flaring reduction. Marginal bear for gas prices but signals EU-US energy policy shift.

themes.headline: EU permits energy-security exemptions for methane rule breaches by oil firms
themes.why_it_matters: Discretionary exemptions on 'security grounds' weaken regulatory certainty; may lower marginal cost of non-compliant gas supply in crisis. Indirect TTF/power support if supply eases.

themes.headline: US strategic petroleum reserve releases 17.5M barrels since late March
themes.why_it_matters: SPR releases suppress crude; lower oil prices reduce fuel-switching risk (coal-to-gas) in European power plants. Indirect downward pressure on European power curve.

themes.headline: Trump administration mobilizes to reopen Strait of Hormuz for shipping
themes.why_it_matters: Hormuz reopening would ease crude/LNG flows from Persian Gulf; lower oil and LNG prices reduce EU power generation costs and storage urgency. Supports bear case for power curve.

watchlist: Hormuz shipping corridor status updates; impact on LNG/crude flows to NW Europe.
Libya production ramp timeline and Italy pipeline utilization rate.
EU methane rule exemption guidelines implementation date and scope.
US-EU trade negotiations (tariff threat) and energy-sector carve-outs.
